rulings.Case Title: COMMUNITY TENANTS' ASSOCIATION ET AL VS. VALSTOCK MANAGEMENT COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Valstock Management Companys Responses To Plaintiffs Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And Form Interrogatories, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 7, 2019 line 4. PLAINTIFF COMMUNITY TENANTS' ASSOCIATION Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Valstock Management Companys Responses To Plaintiffs Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And Form Interrogatories, Set One Hearing Required. If parties have resolved their issues, moving party shall so advise the Court by 9:00 a.m. on January 7, 2020. If the issues are not resolved, Parties to appear and check in with the courtroom clerk at 9:00 a.m. Parties are to proceed to meet and confer and prepare a Joint Separate Statement including: (1) specific request/part of the request Plaintiff believes has not been properly responded to; and (2) specific responses, which Defendant believe satisfies their obligation to respond, stating the date and the substance of the response; stating the bates number, if applicable. The Court will address first 10 requests at 2:30 p.m. The Court will address additional issues on each consecutive business date based on Court's availability (during morning calendar or in the afternoon) starting on January 9, 2020, 10 requests at a time. Parties to appear at 9:30 a.m. on each consecutive business day to proceed with meeting and conferring. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Response to Requests for Admissions, production of Documents and Form Interrogatories (Kelvin Yee) Plaintiffs' Motion to Compel Responses to Request for Admissions, Production of Documents, Form Interrogatories (371 Broadway). Hearing Required. If parties have resolved their issues, moving party shall so advise the Court by 9:00 a.m. on January 7, 2020. If the issues are not resolved, Parties to appear and check in with the courtroom clerk at 9:00 a.m. Parties are to proceed to meet and confer and prepare a Joint Separate Statement including: (1) specific request/part of the request Plaintiff believes has not been properly responded to; and (2) specific responses, which Defendant believe satisfies their obligation to respond, stating the date and the substance of the response; stating the bates number, if applicable. The Court will address first 10 requests at 2:30 p.m. The Court will address additional issues on each consecutive business date based on Court's availability (during morning calendar or in the afternoon) starting on January 9, 2020, 10 requests at a time. Parties to appear at 9:30 a.m. on each consecutive business day to proceed with meeting and conferring. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: 1321 QUESADA AVE 007, LLC VS. TERESITA POBRE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Stay Proceedings And For Tro Staying This Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 7, 2019 line 7. DEFENDANT TERESITA POBRE Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Stay Proceedings And For Tro Staying This Action is DENIED without prejudice. Plaintiff's objections to Defendant's purported evidence submitted with the moving papers are sustained. Evidence improperly submitted on December 31, 2019 (without permission or citation to any authority allowing for such submission) is not considered. The Court will further note that the moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that they may recover title from the third-party purchaser under the circumstances alleged in 571147. If Defendants re-file their request for injunction such authorities shall be included. Further, if Defendants re-file their request for injunction Defendant's shall be prepared for the duration of the injunction: (1) pay all property taxes; (2) pay all taxes, (3) post monthly on the first of each month the Fair Market Value of the property or the contractual mortgage amount, whichever is greater; (4) cover all the property associated expenses; (5) be responsible for any landlord-tenant obligations, if there are any tenants; and (6) not to create any new tenancies. =(501/CFH)
